evidences.content: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) conducted several airstrikes in Beit Jinn, Rif Dimashq Province, on November 28. The 55th Reserve Paratroopers Brigade (210th Division) initially entered Beit Jinn to detain three villagers that the IDF said were al Jamaa al Islamiyya members planning to attack Israeli citizens. Al Jamaa al Islamiyya is a Lebanese Muslim Brotherhood splinter, and its fighters have previously engaged Israeli forces in southern Lebanon. An al Jamaa al Islamiyya representative to the Lebanese Parliament denied that the organization has members in southern Syria. Israeli forces came under fire during the detainment operation. Israeli forces engaged locals in Beit Jinn on November 28, killing 13 villagers and injuring 20 others, according to Syrian media. evidences.title: Shallow Ramparts: Air and Missile Defenses in the June 2025 Israel ...
evidences.content: Since the initial exchanges between the regional rivals in 2024, Israel carefully prepared the battlefield for conflict with Iran, targeting Iran’s most sophisticated air defenses. The failure of the Iranian air defense system during the June 2025 war with Israel was over a year in the making. After True Promise I in April 2024, the Iranian drone and missile attack on Israel in retaliation for the Israeli bombing of the Iranian embassy in Damascus, the Israeli Air Force struck several targets in Iran. The most significant was the radar for the S-300 battery near Isfahan.(#_ftn3) In the wake of True Promise II in October 2024 and the more expansive Israeli Air Force retaliatory strikes, many reports citing anonymous Israeli and American officials stated the remaining Iranian S-300 units [...] (#_ftnref1) Emmanuel Fabian, “Israel has full control of Tehran’s airspace, over 100 missile launchers destroyed, IDF says,” Times of Israel, June 16, 2025, . evidences.title: Iran Update, December 30, 2025
evidences.content: UK-based Amwaj Media reported on December 29 that Iran and Israel have recently exchanged secret messages through Russia to deescalate tensions, citing informed regional and political sources. One unspecified regional source claimed that Netanyahu asked Russia to convey to Iran that Israel does not seek further confrontation with Iran. A separate unspecified source in Tehran claimed that Iran and Israel told a Russian intermediary—believed to be President Vladimir Putin—on an unspecified date that they do not intend to conduct new strikes on the other country. The source reported that Supreme National Security Council Secretary Ali Larijani engaged with the unspecified Russian intermediary~~,~~ rather than the Iranian Foreign Affairs Ministry. They said they aimed to induce regime change in Iran and target its nuclear and ballistic missile programme. The strikes are reported to have caused both military and civilian casualties in Iran . [...] On Iran’s ballistic missile programme, a 2025 report by the US Defense Intelligence Agency had said Iran could potentially “develop a militarily-viable [intercontinental ballistic missile] by 2035”, but did not state if Iran had decided to pursue this. 8 As set out in a US Congressional Research Service report, Iran has long had missiles capable of targeting sites across the Middle East and parts of southern Europe. ## Statements on potential Israeli attack and timing In a statement to the media on 3 March 2026, the US Secretary of State, Marco Rubio, reiterated President Trump’s concerns about Iran’s ballistic and nuclear programmes. evidences.title: Middle East Overview: November 2025 - ACLED
evidences.content: Tensions remain high almost a year after the US- and France-brokered ceasefire between Hezbollah and Israel went into effect on 27 November 2024, amid continued Israel Defense Forces (IDF) daily strikes on Lebanon. The number of Israeli strikes in October remained similar to the month prior, with over 130 shelling and aerial strikes, mainly in the south but also at least 16 in the Bekaa Valley. Raising fears that Israel may be shifting its focus to Lebanon once more after the Gaza ceasefire was reached, the IDF conducted intense airstrikes on 11 October that hit industrial facilities and heavy-equipment yards in the southern village of Msayleh, which killed one Syrian and injured seven other people, including two women. A few days later, on 16 October, the IDF struck cement factories, [...] days later, on 16 October, the IDF struck cement factories, industrial workshops, and a fuel depot. The attacks on industrial facilities in October followed similar strikes in Ansariya in early September. While the IDF continues to claim it struck Hezbollah sites to prevent the group from rebuilding, critics argue that Israel is attempting to cripple the reconstruction and the economic recove...

evidences.title: Iran Says Israeli Energy Facilities Targeted in Retaliatory Drone Strike
evidences.content: What he’s saying: “Iran’s Army Air Force launched suicide drones targeting oil, gas and fuel storage facilities of the Israeli regime in Haifa in response to attacks on Iran’s oil depots.” More of developments: During the 33rd wave of Operation True Promise 4, the Haela satellite communications center south of Tel Aviv was destroyed. The facility functioned as a communications hub linking Israeli air bases with fighter jets. Earlier, missile units of the IRGC Ground Force fired five missiles at the U.S. military headquarters at Harir base in Iraq’s Kurdistan region. Iranian air defense systems intercepted and destroyed two Israeli Heron drones, one over Markazi Province and another Heron TP drone near Tehran. Go deeper: [...] Go deeper: The operations are conducted following the U.S.-Israeli war of aggression against Iran, which began on February 28 with coordinated attacks on Iranian territory. The escalation intensified after the assassination of the Leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei, in an attack on Tehran. Iranian forces have since carried out a series of retaliatory strikes targeting military and strategic infrastructure linked to the aggressor...
